name: Chile
avoid: June–August brings short, cold days in Patagonia and pricier ski-season weekends around central Chile.
stopoverNote: Santiago (SCL) is the unavoidable hub for Easter Island and most long north–south hops, so multi-city Chile trips usually backtrack through the capital at least once.

cities.name: Santiago
cities.notes: Historic core, Lastarria cafés, Cerro San Cristóbal views and Chile’s best-connected food-and-metro base.

cities.name: Valparaíso
cities.notes: Colorful hills, working port, graffiti lanes, funiculars and Pablo Neruda’s sea-facing house museum.

cities.name: San Pedro de Atacama
cities.notes: Adobe desert village for moonlike valleys, salt lagoons, geysers and some of South America’s clearest night skies.

cities.name: Puerto Natales
cities.notes: Gateway town for Torres del Paine, Grey Glacier sailings and classic Patagonian wind-and-mountain scenery.

cities.name: Pucón
cities.notes: Volcano-and-lake adventure base with hot springs, rafting, Araucanía forest trails and a relaxed backpacker feel.

cities.name: Easter Island
cities.notes: Remote Polynesian island of moai statues, quarry sites, crater rims and the Hanga Roa seaside base.

itinerary.sim: Entel and WOM usually give the smoothest prepaid coverage in Santiago and across the main mainland route; buy the SIM in Santiago, then download offline maps before Atacama, Patagonia, and Easter Island where data can get patchy or slow.

Santiago.name: Santiago
Santiago.note: Stay in Lastarria or Providencia, load a Bip! card for Metro/bus rides, and use Cabify late at night instead of negotiating street taxis.

activities.name: Centropuerto or TurBus airport bus from SCL into central Santiago
Walk Plaza de Armas, Santiago Cathedral, Paseo Ahumada and La Moneda exterior
Museo Chileno de Arte Precolombino
Cerro San Cristóbal funicular and cable car circuit
Breakfast stroll through La Vega Central and Mercado Central
Climb Cerro Santa Lucía for old-city lookouts
Sky Costanera observation deck
Bellavista evening for murals, bars, and live music

Valparaíso.name: Valparaíso
Valparaíso.note: Go as a day trip or one-night stay from Santiago; the city is steep, so pack light and use funiculars or quick Ubers between cerros after dark.

days.theme: Street Art Hills, Port Views & Neruda House

activities.name: TurBus or Pullman Bus ride from Santiago to Valparaíso
Cerro Alegre and Cerro Concepción self-guided mural walk
Ascensor Reina Victoria and Paseo Gervasoni viewpoints
La Sebastiana (Pablo Neruda house museum)

San Pedro de Atacama.name: San Pedro de Atacama
San Pedro de Atacama.note: The town sits around 2,400 m, so hydrate hard, go easy on alcohol the first night, and keep sunrise tours for after you’ve had at least one acclimatisation evening.

days.theme: Arrival, Desert Town & Valle de la Luna

activities.name: Shared shuttle or bus from Calama Airport to San Pedro
Walk Caracoles street, the church plaza, and artisan lanes
Valle de la Luna official entry
Piedra del Coyote (Mirador de Kari) for sunset over the Cordillera de la Sal
Salar de Atacama and Laguna Chaxa flamingo reserve day tour
Piedras Rojas and Miscanti–Miñiques altiplano lagoons excursion
Socaire village lunch stop
Stargazing astronomy tour after dinner
El Tatio Geysers entry
Pre-dawn El Tatio guided transport from San Pedro
Machuca village stop on the return
Afternoon at Puritama Hot Springs

Puerto Natales.name: Puerto Natales
Puerto Natales.note: Book park entry online before you go, carry full windproof layers even on sunny days, and keep a spare weather-flex day if Base Torres is a priority hike.

activities.name: Costanera, Muelle Histórico and artisan shops around Plaza de Armas
Cueva del Milodón Natural Monument
Bus station recon for tomorrow’s Torres del Paine departure
Sunset over Última Esperanza Sound
Torres del Paine National Park entry (up to 3 days, foreign adult)
Round-trip bus Puerto Natales ↔ Laguna Amarga / Pudeto
Salto Grande, Lago Pehoé, Cuernos del Paine and Mirador Nordenskjöld stops
Grey Lake beach walk or optional Grey III navigation

days.theme: Hike or Sail Day in Patagonia

activities.name: Base Torres shuttle-supported hiking day or guided full-day trek
Torres del Paine park upgrade for stays over 3 days
Alternative Balmaceda & Serrano glacier boat trip from Puerto Natales
Back in town for craft beer and king-crab dinner

Pucón.name: Pucón
Pucón.note: Pucón is easy to do without a car, but adventure tours fill up in summer; reserve volcano slots or hot-spring transfers a day or two ahead.

activities.name: Guided Villarrica Volcano ascent with crampons and gear
Alternative chairlift / snowline viewpoint if summit conditions close
Walk Playa Grande and the main lakeshore promenade
Evening craft-beer stop in central Pucón
Ojos del Caburgua waterfalls
Huerquehue National Park day hike or lake circuit
Late soak at Termas Geométricas
Optional Trancura River rafting

Easter Island.name: Easter Island
Easter Island.note: Base yourself in Hanga Roa, keep cash for smaller family-run places, and hire a local guide if you want the deeper archaeological context at the quarry and ceremonial sites.

days.theme: Rano Raraku, Tongariki & East Coast Moai

activities.name: Rapa Nui National Park digital pass
Rano Raraku quarry visit
Ahu Tongariki sunrise or late-afternoon platform stop
Ahu Akahanga and Puna Pau circuit by guided van
Orongo ceremonial village and Rano Kau crater rim
Anakena beach and Ahu Nau Nau moai platform
Half-day scooter or car rental for independent coastal stops
Tahai ceremonial complex for sunset near Hanga Roa

connections.method: Frequent TurBus or Pullman Bus service from Terminal Alameda / Terminal Sur, about 1 hr 45 min to 2 hr 15 min
Bus back to Santiago, then LATAM / SKY / JetSMART flight to Calama (CJC) plus shared shuttle to San Pedro
Shuttle to Calama, then LATAM / SKY / JetSMART via Santiago to Puerto Natales or Punta Arenas, plus final bus if needed
Bus to Punta Arenas or direct airport transfer when seasonal flights run, then flight via Santiago to Temuco and final bus to Pucón
Bus to Temuco, flight to Santiago, then LATAM’s Santiago–Mataveri (IPC) service to Easter Island

extras: Chile is long rather than wide: use buses only for Santiago–Valparaíso and maybe Santiago–Pucón; for Atacama, Patagonia, and Easter Island, flights save huge amounts of time.
Torres del Paine entry is bought online in advance via Pases Parques/CONAF, and summer availability can tighten around Christmas, New Year, and February.
San Pedro tours often quote transport first and then add park cash/online entries separately, so read the inclusions carefully before paying a deposit.
Easter Island is one of the priciest domestic add-ons in South America; book the LATAM sector early and expect meals plus vehicle rentals to cost more than mainland Chile.
Cards work well in Santiago and larger hotels, but keep some pesos for bus terminals, smaller cafés, and family-run places in Puerto Natales, Pucón, and Hanga Roa.
If you have under 10 days, keep Santiago, Valparaíso, San Pedro de Atacama, and Puerto Natales as the core route, then treat Pucón and Easter Island as separate add-on trips.

Puerto Natales: Torres del Paine Cuernos del Paine
